text:
Once upon a quiet mountain town, there lived a curious cat named Felix. Felix had a talent for discovering hidden treasures and secrets, and the townsfolk always had tales to tell about curious things they’d stumbled upon.

One spring morning, while lounging on the windowsill of Lady Agnes’ parlour, Felix heard an unusual rustling at the foot of the cottage. He cocked his ears and purred in attention. Following the sound, he stumbled across a tiny door in the wall. Pushing it open, a miniature stairwell revealed itself, which in turn led to a tiny doorway into the attic.

With nary a soul in sight, Felix climbed into the attic, finding it as grand as it was small. He explored, discovering a collection of vintage postcards from far-off places—the corners filled with dust, but promising adventures. As he pondered his next discovery, his paw brushed against a peculiar-looking keyhole tucked in a dusty shelf.

Curiosity panged, Felix
